originalTitle: The impossible dream of the universal remote
summary: You don't really ever have to explain why a universal remote is a good idea. You have a bunch of stuff that needs controlling; this thing controls them all. Many companies have set out to build a product worthy of this idea, and one product came much closer than most. It was called the Harmony, […]
content: You don’t really ever have to explain why a universal remote is a good idea. You have a bunch of stuff that needs controlling; this thing controls them all. Many companies have set out to build a product worthy of this idea, and one product came much closer than most. It was called the Harmony, and for many years it was the best universal remote on the market. Maybe the only one that mattered. And still, even the Harmony couldn’t make it work. On this episode of Version History, we tell the story of the Harmony. The Verge’s David Pierce, Nilay Patel, and John Higgins are joined by Matt Rogers, the CEO of Mill and former co-founder of Nest, to reckon with two decades of buttons and touchscreens. The product begins as the Easy Zapper, takes off, sells to Logitech, expands like crazy for a number of years… and then begins to fade. Sure, you could argue smart TVs and integrated entertainment systems just made the whole idea irrelevant. But there’s something about the universal remote that feels as enticing as ever. So we try to figure out why. This is the first episode of the fourth season of Version History. (We are so back.) For the next several weeks, we’re telling a bunch of stories about the smart home, from Hue lights to Keurig coffee to the viral sensation that was The Clapper. Here’s how to get every episode, and all our other fun stuff, as soon as it drops: * The Version History podcast feedThe best universal remote you can buy | The Verge * The Version History YouTube channel * Our TikTok and Instagram accounts If you’re a Verge subscriber, you can also get access to Version History (and all our other podcasts) with no ads. All you have to do is visit your account settings. If you want to know more about the history and legacy of the Harmony, here are a few links to get you started: * From The Washington Post: Control Issues * The original Easy Zapper website, on Harmony launch day * The first remote * From CRN: Harmonic Convergence At Hand * Harmony remotes are fading in relevance as streaming takes over, says Logitech CEO * From PC World: Logitech is dropping support for its oldest Harmony remotes
chineseTitle: 万能遥控器的不可能梦想
chineseSummary: 您实际上不必解释为什么通用遥控器是个好主意。你有很多东西需要控制；这个东西控制着他们所有人。许多公司已经着手打造一款符合这一理念的产品，其中一款产品比大多数产品更接近这一理念。它被称为和谐，[...]
chineseContent: 您实际上不必解释为什么通用遥控器是个好主意。你有很多东西需要控制；这个东西控制着他们所有人。许多公司已经着手打造一款符合这一理念的产品，其中一款产品比大多数产品更接近这一理念。它被称为 Harmony，多年来一直是市场上最好的通用遥控器。也许是唯一重要的。然而，即使是 Harmony 也无法让它发挥作用。在本期版本历史中，我们讲述 Harmony 的故事。 The Verge 的 David Pierce、Nilay Patel 和 John Higgins 以及 Mill 首席执行官、Nest 前联合创始人 Matt Rogers 一起回顾了二十年来的按钮和触摸屏。该产品从 Easy Zapper 开始，起飞，卖给罗技，疯狂扩张了好几年……然后开始衰落。当然，你可能会说智能电视和集成娱乐系统让整个想法变得无关紧要。但通用遥控器还是一如既往地具有吸引力。所以我们试图找出原因。这是版本历史第四季的第一集。 （我们回来了。）在接下来的几周里，我们将讲述一系列关于智能家居的故事，从 Hue 灯到 Keurig 咖啡，再到风靡一时的 The Clapper。以下是如何在每一集以及所有其他有趣的内容发布后立即获取它： * 版本历史播客源您可以购买的最好的通用遥控器 | The Verge * 版本历史记录 YouTube 频道 * 我们的 TikTok 和 Instagram 帐户 如果您是 Verge 订阅者，您还可以访问版本历史记录（以及我们所有其他播客），且没有广告。您所要做的就是访问您的帐户设置。如果您想了解更多关于和谐的历史和遗产，这里有一些链接
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
